notes: v363 — ↩ UNDO A SALE IS BACK (this time it can't wreck your balance). You asked for it: a trailing stop sold you out and you wanted to reverse it. There's now an ↩ Undo button on every row of your 📜 History — it puts the shares back in your holdings and takes the cash back out. The key change: the OLD undo blindly clawed back the sale money even if you'd already spent it on other buys, and THAT is what kept driving your cash negative. Now, if the money from a sale is already tied up in other positions, Undo won't run — it tells you to free up about $X first (cancel a pending order or sell a holding, or use ➕ add cash), then undo. Restored shares are paused from auto-selling so a rule can't sell them straight back. On BOTH customer and beta. Hard-refresh (Ctrl+F5) to load v363.
